summary: Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to split several larger .data symbols in the gm_1601 region into smaller objects and mark many of them as data:string, with a stated purpose of preparing for future file splitting. The change is metadata/config-only and does not decompile code directly, but improves symbol granularity around embedded .c filename strings and adjacent data.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, several broad .data labels in the gm_1601 area were resized and split into more precise labels.

Changed lbl_803D6878 from a 0x54 byte object to a 0x20 byte object, then introduced adjacent symbols lbl_803D6898, lbl_803D68CC, lbl_803D6900, lbl_803D6934, lbl_803D6940, lbl_803D6974, lbl_803D69A0, and lbl_803D69CC with smaller sizes; several were tagged data:string.
Changed lbl_803D7910 from size 0xC0 to size 0x70 and added lbl_803D7980 and lbl_803D7994 as string data objects.
Marked lbl_803D7A40 as data:string while preserving its 0x10 size.
Changed lbl_803D8B88 from size 0x150 to size 0xE8 and added local string labels lbl_803D8C70, lbl_803D8C80, lbl_803D8CB0, lbl_803D8CBC, and lbl_803D8CCC.
Marked lbl_803D9F5C as data:string while preserving its 0xC size.
smart_moves: The PR avoided renaming unknown labels without stronger evidence; it only refined symbol boundaries, size metadata, scope, and data:string annotations.

Splitting large anonymous .data ranges into individual string/object symbols makes later source-file splitting less risky because embedded filename strings and neighboring constants can be attributed separately.
Local scope was preserved or added for the lbl_803D8C70 through lbl_803D8CCC string labels, preventing unnecessarily global symbol exposure.
The change kept existing jump table annotations intact while refining nearby data, reducing the chance of disturbing already-understood control-flow metadata.
decomp_lessons: Before splitting a source file, mark embedded filename strings and other fine-grained data objects in symbols.txt so generated assembly/data boundaries align with the eventual C translation units.
Large anonymous .data objects may hide several independent strings; use exact address and size splits to expose them instead of treating the whole range as one blob.
For filename-related metadata work, data:string annotations are useful even when the final semantic symbol names are not yet known.
Config-only symbol boundary refinements can be a preparatory PR for later decompilation or file-splitting work.
naming_conventions: This PR retained address-based placeholder labels such as lbl_803D6898 rather than introducing semantic names without clear evidence.
String data was indicated through the symbols.txt comment convention data:string.
Local-only data used the symbols.txt convention scope:local, as seen on lbl_803D8C70, lbl_803D8C80, lbl_803D8CB0, lbl_803D8CBC, and lbl_803D8CCC.
Jump table labels retained the jumptable_803D7A50 and jumptable_803D7A70 naming pattern.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Refine .data object boundaries by shrinking oversized symbols and inserting new labels at the discovered internal addresses.
Use data:string tags for embedded strings so future matching and file splitting can distinguish text literals from opaque data.
Preserve existing symbol addresses and only adjust sizes/annotations where the diff gives clear new boundaries.
No evidence in the slice of assembly rewrite tactics, compiler matching tricks, or C code changes; this was a symbols.txt metadata update.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body stated the purpose succinctly: "For future file splitting."
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR or commit that actually splits gm_1601 files after these filename-string annotations.
Search for gm_1601 references near addresses 0x803D6878 through 0x803D9F80 to identify which C filenames these strings correspond to.
Check whether any of the newly marked lbl_803D68xx/lbl_803D8Cxx string labels later received semantic names.
